text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY -- OUTPATIENT VISIT NOTE
Attending Oncologist: [Attending Physician]
Fellow/Resident: [Trainee]
Visit Type: Established Patient -- Systemic Therapy Response Assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Margaret Chen is a 63-year-old female with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung carcinoma (adenocarcinoma) presenting for response assessment following completion of cycle 4 of carboplatin/pemetrexed induction chemotherapy and transition to pemetrexed maintenance. She is accompanied today by her husband.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Chen carries a known diagnosis of stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (left upper lobe primary, with right adrenal metastasis and three brain metastases identified at time of systemic progression). Her disease course to date has been as follows:

She was originally diagnosed with stage IIIB (T3N3M0) left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma with bulky mediastinal and ipsilateral supraclavicular lymphadenopathy at age 62. Molecular profiling revealed an EGFR L858R mutation and a co-occurring TP53 R175H mutation, along with CDKN2A homozygous deletion and NKX2-1 amplification. PD-L1 TPS was 15%. TMB was low (4 mut/Mb). She was initiated on osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as definitive systemic therapy, achieving an excellent partial response at 3 months (primary mass 4.2 cm --> 2.1 cm) and a continued deep partial response at 9 months (mass 1.5 cm). She experienced manageable EGFR inhibitor-class toxicities including grade 2 acneiform rash (treated with topical clindamycin and doxycycline) and grade 1 paronychia.

At 14 months on osimertinib, repeat CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed disease progression with growth of the left upper lobe residual mass (1.5 cm --> 2.8 cm), new left hilar lymphadenopathy, and a new 1.1 cm right adrenal nodule. MRI brain simultaneously revealed three new enhancing brain metastases in the right frontal (1.4 cm), right parietal (0.8 cm), and left cerebellar hemispheres (0.6 cm). She was neurologically intact at that time with no symptoms. Osimertinib was held and a CT-guided right adrenal core needle biopsy was performed, which confirmed metastatic adenocarcinoma of lung origin. Repeat NGS on the adrenal specimen demonstrated retention of the EGFR L858R mutation (AF 44%), acquisition of an EGFR T790M resistance mutation (AF 31%), and a newly emergent PIK3CA E545K mutation (AF 19%), consistent with acquired resistance to osimertinib. No EGFR C797S or MET amplification was identified.

She underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to all three brain metastases using the Gamma Knife Perfexion platform (right frontal 20 Gy x1, right parietal 20 Gy x1, left cerebellar 18 Gy x1), which she tolerated with grade 2 post-SRS fatigue and grade 1 headache requiring a short dexamethasone course. Following recovery from SRS, she was initiated on second-line systemic therapy with carboplatin (AUC 5) plus pemetrexed (500 mg/m2) every 21 days, with standard folic acid/vitamin B12 supplementation per pemetrexed protocol.

During cycle 2, she developed grade 3 neutropenia (ANC 0.7 x 10^9/L) with grade 2 nausea, requiring a one-week cycle delay, filgrastim support for 5 days, and antiemetic optimization. She recovered appropriately and was treated at full dose from cycle 3 onward with G-CSF primary prophylaxis. She has now completed 4 cycles total of carboplatin/pemetrexed induction.

Today's visit is for response assessment following 3-cycle restaging imaging (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain), and to discuss transition to pemetrexed maintenance chemotherapy.

Pertinent interval symptoms:
- Fatigue: Persistent grade 1, manageable, not limiting ADLs
- Dyspnea: Mild exertional, ECOG unchanged at PS 1
- Nausea: Grade 1 intermittently, well controlled with prochlorperazine PRN
- Appetite: Mildly reduced; husband assisting with caloric supplementation at home
- Neurological: No headaches, no focal weakness, no ataxia, no visual changes since SRS -- she has remained neurologically completely intact
- Cough: Mild, nonproductive, unchanged from baseline
- No hemoptysis
- No chest pain, no pleuritic symptoms
- No bone pain
- No new skin rash (prior EGFR inhibitor-associated rash fully resolved since discontinuation of osimertinib)
- Paronychia (left index finger) also resolved
- Bowel habits: Regular, no diarrhea on current regimen

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Fatigue as noted above. No fever, chills, or unintentional weight loss (weight is stable at today's measurement, see below).
Pulmonary: Mild baseline exertional dyspnea, stable. No pleuritic pain. No significant change in cough.
Neurological: No headaches, no dizziness, no focal deficits. No cognitive changes noted by patient or husband.
Gastrointestinal: Mild nausea, well controlled. No vomiting, no mucositis, no constipation, no diarrhea.
Hematologic: No bruising, no significant bleeding. No febrile episodes since last visit.
Genitourinary: No hematuria, no dysuria, normal frequency.
Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain. No arthralgias.
Skin: Mild residual dryness at prior EGFR rash sites, no active acneiform lesions, no rash.
Cardiovascular: No palpitations, no edema.
Endocrine: No symptoms of hyperglycemia; glucose management was not a concern on carboplatin/pemetrexed (this was an issue on prior EGFR inhibitor regimens and will be monitored if/when PI3K-directed agents are introduced).

---

PAST ONCOLOGIC HISTORY (SUMMARY)
- Stage IIIB (T3N3M0) left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant, diagnosed age 62
- Progressed to stage IV with systemic and CNS metastases on osimertinib at 14 months
- Acquired resistance: EGFR T790M + PIK3CA E545K
- SRS x3 brain metastases (Gamma Knife)
- Now on carboplatin/pemetrexed x4 cycles (cycle 3+ with G-CSF prophylaxis)

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Hypertension (well controlled on amlodipine)
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus (on metformin; HbA1c last checked at 7.1%)
- Former smoker: 25 pack-year history, quit 8 years ago

SURGICAL HISTORY
- CT-guided left upper lobe core needle biopsy (at initial diagnosis)
- CT-guided right adrenal core needle biopsy (at progression)

ALLERGIES
- No known drug allergies

MEDICATIONS
1. Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 IV every 21 days (transitioning to maintenance after today)
2. Folic acid 400 mcg orally once daily (pemetrexed support)
3. Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM every 9 weeks (pemetrexed support)
4. Dexamethasone 4 mg orally twice daily on day -1, day of, and day +1 of pemetrexed (skin prophylaxis)
5. Ondansetron 8 mg orally PRN nausea (post-chemotherapy)
6. Prochlorperazine 10 mg orally PRN nausea (breakthrough)
7. Filgrastim 480 mcg subcutaneously daily x 5 days starting day 4 post-chemotherapy (primary prophylaxis cycle 3+)
8. Amlodipine 10 mg orally once daily (hypertension)
9. Metformin 1000 mg orally twice daily (type 2 diabetes)
10. Loperamide 2 mg PRN (as needed, not recently used)

SOCIAL HISTORY
- Retired accountant, lives at home with husband who is engaged and supportive
- Non-smoker (former, quit 8 years ago)
- Rare alcohol use (1-2 drinks/week, patient-reported)
- No illicit drug use
- Functionally independent; husband present at all oncology visits

FAMILY HISTORY
- Mother: breast cancer, diagnosed in her 70s
- Father: coronary artery disease
- No known family history of lung cancer or other thoracic malignancies
- No known hereditary cancer syndrome

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital Signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 74 bpm, RR 16, SpO2 96% on room air, Temp 36.7 C. Weight 62.4 kg (stable from last visit, down 1.2 kg from pre-chemotherapy baseline -- clinically insignificant). Height 163 cm. BSA 1.68 m2 (used for dosing).

General: Ms. Chen is a well-appearing, well-nourished female, appearing her stated age, in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x4. Accompanied by her husband.

HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. No alopecia (mild thinning, consistent with chemotherapy). Oropharynx moist, no mucositis, no oral ulcerations. Sclerae non-icteric.

Lymph Nodes: No palpable cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Prior left supraclavicular lymphadenopathy, which was palpable at diagnosis, is no longer detectable.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1/S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.

Pulmonary: Mildly decreased breath sounds at the left upper lobe, unchanged from prior visits. No egophony, no crackles. Right lung field clear. No dullness to percussion in the right base (pleural effusion present on prior imaging was right-sided -- not detectable on exam today, suggesting it was small; confirmed small on CT below).

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly, no splenomegaly palpated. No abdominal masses. Normal bowel sounds.

Extremities: No clubbing, no cyanosis. No lower extremity edema. Nail beds with mild residual changes from prior paronychia, healing appropriately.

Skin: Mild residual xerosis over face and upper chest from prior EGFR inhibitor-associated rash -- no active acneiform lesions, no new rash.

Neurological: Alert and oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 bilateral upper and lower extremities. Sensation intact to light touch. Coordination: finger-nose-finger intact bilaterally, no ataxia. Gait steady and symmetric -- notable given prior left cerebellar SRS target. Deep tendon reflexes 2+ throughout.

Performance Status: ECOG 1.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (CURRENT VISIT / MOST RECENT PRE-CYCLE LABS)
CBC:
- WBC: 5.8 x 10^9/L (ref 4.0-11.0)
- ANC: 2.9 x 10^9/L (recovered from prior G3 neutropenia)
- Hemoglobin: 10.9 g/dL (mild anemia, stable, likely chemotherapy-related)
- Platelets: 188 x 10^9/L (adequate)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Creatinine: 0.78 mg/dL; eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2 (adequate for continued pemetrexed dosing)
- BUN: 14 mg/dL
- AST: 24 U/L, ALT: 31 U/L (within normal limits)
- Alkaline phosphatase: 88 U/L (normal)
- Total bilirubin: 0.6 mg/dL
- Albumin: 3.6 g/dL (low-normal; nutritional supplementation discussed)
- Glucose: 118 mg/dL (fasting, mildly elevated; consistent with T2DM, no change in management required)
- Sodium, potassium, magnesium, calcium: All within normal limits

Other:
- HbA1c: 7.1% (stable; diabetes reasonably controlled)
- LDH: 196 U/L (normal)
- Homocysteine: 9.2 umol/L (normal; confirms adequate B12/folate supplementation on pemetrexed protocol)

ECG: Not repeated today (last QTc 432 ms at 3-month mark on osimertinib; not indicated with current platinum/pemetrexed regimen).

---

IMAGING RESULTS

CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast (3-cycle response assessment -- reviewed in detail today):
Left upper lobe mass: The primary lesion measures 2.8 cm in longest diameter, unchanged from the CT obtained at the time of progression on osimertinib. There is no appreciable growth and no appreciable shrinkage -- the mass has been stable across all three completed cycles. The lesion maintains its irregular, spiculated morphology with the same distribution of associated post-obstructive changes in the left upper lobe. No new pulmonary nodules identified in either lung field.
Mediastinum/Lymph Nodes: Previously noted left hilar lymphadenopathy and residual mediastinal adenopathy appear stable to mildly decreased in size from the progression scan. No new mediastinal, hilar, or axillary adenopathy identified.
Right Adrenal: The right adrenal nodule measures 1.1 cm, unchanged since it was first identified at progression. No new adrenal lesions.
Liver: No hepatic lesions identified. The liver is normal in size and attenuation. No biliary dilation.
Other Abdominal Organs: Pancreas, spleen, kidneys unremarkable. No ascites.
Pleura: Small right-sided pleural effusion previously noted appears stable to minimally decreased; no loculation. No new pleural nodularity.
Bones: No new lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified.
Overall Impression: Stable disease. No progression, no new sites of involvement. The left upper lobe mass, left hilar adenopathy, and right adrenal nodule are all maintained at the same sizes as on the prior study. This represents a best response of stable disease on carboplatin/pemetrexed induction.

MRI Brain with and without Gadolinium (post-SRS surveillance, 3-cycle mark):
All three prior SRS treatment sites show expected post-treatment change. The right frontal lesion (treated at 20 Gy x1) demonstrates a well-defined area of T1 enhancement with surrounding T2/FLAIR signal changes, in keeping with treatment effect/radiation necrosis; no concerning features for active progression. The right parietal and left cerebellar lesions show treatment-related signal changes with no residual enhancement suspicious for viable tumor. No new enhancing lesions are identified anywhere in the brain. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No hydrocephalus. Mass effect is absent.
Intracranial impression: Excellent response to SRS. No new intracranial metastatic disease. Stable post-treatment changes at all three sites.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Chen is a 63-year-old woman with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, previously treated with osimertinib (14 months, achieved partial response before acquired T790M/PIK3CA E545K resistance), now on second-line carboplatin/pemetrexed with SRS to oligometastatic brain disease. She is completing her 4-cycle induction course today and is being transitioned to pemetrexed maintenance.

1. Disease Status -- Stable Disease on Carboplatin/Pemetrexed:
Imaging is reassuring. The systemic disease is stable -- the lung primary and adrenal nodule have not grown, and there are no new sites of disease in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. While we have not achieved a radiographic response (shrinkage) with platinum doublet chemotherapy, disease stabilization in this setting represents a meaningful clinical benefit given the aggressive behavior of this EGFR-mutant tumor at acquired resistance. Intracranially, the SRS results are excellent -- all three treated lesions show post-treatment change consistent with effective ablation, and there are no new brain metastases. Neurologically, she remains completely intact, which is a high priority.

I discussed today's imaging with Ms. Chen and her husband in detail, explaining that the scans show the cancer is "holding steady" on chemotherapy -- it has not grown or spread anywhere new, which is a positive result. I also reassured her that the brain treatment continues to look effective.

2. Transition to Pemetrexed Maintenance:
Given stable disease after 4 cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed induction, she will now transition to pemetrexed (Alimta) maintenance monotherapy, 500 mg/m2 IV every 21 days, per standard of care for non-squamous NSCLC. This is consistent with published data (PARAMOUNT trial) supporting pemetrexed continuation maintenance following platinum doublet induction in the non-squamous setting.

Carboplatin will be discontinued. She will continue:
- Pemetrexed 500 mg/m2 IV every 21 days
- Folic acid 400 mcg daily orally
- Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM every 9 weeks
- Dexamethasone skin prophylaxis on days -1/0/+1

G-CSF primary prophylaxis: Given prior grade 3 neutropenia on the doublet regimen, I will continue filgrastim 480 mcg SC x5 days starting day 4 post-pemetrexed as a precaution for the initial maintenance cycles, with intent to reassess after cycle 2 of maintenance. Single-agent pemetrexed carries a substantially lower myelosuppression burden than the doublet, and we may be able to de-escalate G-CSF support.

3. Hematologic Monitoring:
Mild anemia (Hgb 10.9 g/dL) is noted -- clinically insignificant at this time, patient is not symptomatic from an anemia standpoint. Will recheck CBC with cycle 1 of maintenance (cycle 5 overall). Iron studies deferred for now unless anemia worsens.

4. Nutritional Status:
Albumin mildly low at 3.6 g/dL. Weight has been stable but mildly reduced from pre-chemo baseline. Husband active in ensuring adequate oral intake at home. I reinforced the importance of caloric density in her diet. Will re-refer to oncology dietitian for a check-in visit. No indication for enteral support at this time.

5. Diabetes / Glucose Management:
Fasting glucose 118 mg/dL, HbA1c 7.1% -- diabetes remains reasonably controlled on metformin. No change to diabetes management at this time. When/if she transitions to investigational therapy in the future (particularly given the PIK3CA E545K resistance mutation, which may make her a candidate for PI3K inhibitor-based clinical trial), hyperglycemia will become an important management concern given PI3K pathway inhibition effects on insulin signaling.

6. CNS Surveillance:
Will continue MRI brain surveillance every 3 months given history of multiple brain metastases and the known CNS propensity of EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Next MRI brain scheduled to coincide with next systemic CT restaging (after 2-3 maintenance cycles).

7. Clinical Trial Consideration -- Future Direction:
I had a frank goals-of-care and treatment planning discussion with Ms. Chen and her husband today. We discussed the trajectory of her disease, acknowledging that while the current chemotherapy regimen is providing disease control, her underlying resistance profile (T790M + PIK3CA E545K co-mutation) is a recognized high-risk biological feature associated with limited durability of platinum-based therapy. I discussed with her that a Phase I/II clinical trial targeting this specific resistance profile (involving a novel EGFR inhibitor and a PI3K alpha-selective inhibitor) may be an option at the time of next progression on pemetrexed maintenance. I have already made contact with the clinical trials team. She expressed strong interest in trial participation if/when she becomes eligible. No formal enrollment or screening initiated at this time.

8. Supportive Care:
- Antiemetics: Continue ondansetron and prochlorperazine PRN. Given that carboplatin (the highly emetogenic component) is now discontinued, overall emetic burden with pemetrexed maintenance should diminish significantly.
- Pain: No pain issues at present.
- Psychosocial: Patient and husband appear to be coping well. She remains engaged and optimistic. Social work follow-up as needed; patient declined formal referral at this time but contact information provided.

9. Follow-up:
She will return in 3 weeks for cycle 1 of pemetrexed maintenance (cycle 5 overall), with pre-cycle CBC, BMP, and LFTs. Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain planned after completion of 2-3 maintenance cycles. Oncology dietitian referral placed today.

Time spent with patient: approximately 55 minutes, of which more than 50% was counseling and coordination of care.

Electronically signed by: [Attending Oncologist, MD]
Dictated and reviewed by: [Fellow, MD]

---
CC: Primary care physician, radiation oncology
